title: Plagiocheilus bogotensis
intro: Plagiocheilus bogotensis, also known as the Bogotá passionflower or passionflower of Bogotá, is a plant species native to the Andean region of South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and inflammation, due to its calming and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Plagiocheilus bogotensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The plant is commonly prepared as an infusion, tincture, or dried extract, and is often used to make teas and herbal supplements to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
taxonomy: Plagiocheilus bogotensis is a species of plant in the family Gentianaceae. This plant is commonly found in the mountainous regions of South America. The classification of Plagiocheilus bogotensis includes being in the kingdom Plantae, and the order Gentianales. It is further classified into the family Gentianaceae and the genus Plagiocheilus. Plagiocheilus bogotensis is also known as a species of gentian.
